page: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, as evidenced by its high scores in multi-threaded stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests. Memory performance is highly efficient, with read-only small-block cached bandwidth reaching 30,925.21 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 1,169.67 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the average tier. While prompt processing for large language models performs strongly, text generation speeds are a notable tradeoff, registering as average for medium and large models, and poor for small models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture offers high resource density and direct hardware access, making it suitable for large-scale deployments that require massive parallel processing without virtualization overhead. The high memory bandwidth and multi-core performance make this server well-suited for heavy database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks. It is also appropriate for memory-intensive general-purpose workloads, though applications requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration will need to look at alternative configurations.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 384 vCPUs across 192 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, a 480 MB L3 cache, and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and strong database operations. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation speeds represent a performance tradeoff, particularly for smaller models. This server is highly cost-efficient for large-scale, non-virtualized workloads requiring massive parallel processing and high memory throughput. It is best suited for enterprise database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal server with 384 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, optimized for high-throughput multi-core workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal server featuring 384 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking for multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform delivering 384 vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores
1536 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity
Top-tier multi-core processing, database operations, and memory bandwidth
Best suited for enterprise databases, software compilation, and web serving
Tradeoffs include average single-core performance and no local storage
